key_insights.insight: Entrepreneurship is about solving a problem with the least amount of resources and the highest amount of output.
key_insights.quote: Entrepreneurship is fundamentally, you have a problem and you are solving in a way that uses the least amount of resources, but has the highest amount of output.

key_insights.insight: The cost of failure in starting a business is the lowest it has ever been, making it easier to try new ideas.
key_insights.quote: The cost of failure is as close to zero as it's ever been right now.

key_insights.insight: Passion is not the only catalyst for starting a business; desperation or necessity can be a powerful driver.
key_insights.quote: One is passion and the other is disparity or being desperate. And actually I think entrepreneurs by necessity are a big part of the greatest companies ever been created.

key_insights.insight: To be truly exceptional, one should develop a 'spiky' skillset - a deep mastery of a few unobvious, complementary skills within an industry rather than trying to be well-rounded.
key_insights.quote: Maybe I don't have to be well-rounded. Maybe I can actually be spiky. And instead of actually trying to be a well-rounded leader, maybe I can focus on sharpening that point and sharpening that point over and over again.

key_insights.insight: When building a team, seek out people with complementary skills rather than those who are similar to yourself.
key_insights.quote: Don't start a company with the person sitting next to you. Walk across the street, go to a different faculty, go to the faculty of arts, go to the faculty of philosophy, go to the faculty of engineering, and find someone there to start a company with.

key_insights.insight: Entrepreneurs should invest in building anti-fragile systems and mindsets, meaning they should embrace challenges and use them to become stronger and more resilient.
key_insights.quote: Shopify is fundamentally an anti-fragile company. And I think your companies are the same way. You guys invite what call it pain, call it challenge, because ultimately you want to come out the other side better.

key_insights.insight: Vulnerability is a strength, not a weakness, and sharing your struggles with the right people can lead to greater personal and professional growth.
key_insights.quote: Vulnerability for the right people...it shows strength. It does not show weakness.

key_insights.insight: Find a company whose mission resonates with your own life's mission.
key_insights.quote: But I think you do your best work when the mission of the company deeply reflects your own life's mission.

key_insights.insight: Effective prioritization involves diarizing must-have activities and sticking to the schedule.
key_insights.quote: The things that I really, really care about, I diarize and I stick to that diarization.

key_insights.insight: Successful content creators don't just slap their brand on existing products; they create better versions of existing products that cater to their audience.
key_insights.quote: I would definitely put one of those millions of dollars into the hands of a creator who's on the precipice of figuring out what exactly does my audience like and then creating a better version of that.

actionable_advice.advice: When facing multiple competitors, focus on doing it better by having deep empathy for your customers and adding disproportionate value at every step.
actionable_advice.how_to: Engage directly with customers, understand their needs, and use technology to make things better for them at every stage of the process.

actionable_advice.advice: When seeking mentorship, identify individuals who excel in specific verticals of your life (parenting, marriage, business) and seek their individual guidance.
actionable_advice.how_to: Create a 'personal board of advisors' by seeking counsel from different people who are strong in different areas of life.

actionable_advice.advice: Develop a personal culture that fosters constant evolution and improvement.
actionable_advice.how_to: Invite new ideas and perspectives, create an atmosphere where people constantly challenge each other in a positive way, and ensure the product is continually evolving uniquely and positively.

actionable_advice.advice: Find a 'tribe' of individuals who are at a similar stage in their entrepreneurial journey to share war stories, learn tactics and form a personal board of directors.
actionable_advice.how_to: Look at Reddit, YouTube, and other social media. Engage with the power users and try to connect with them either online or in person.

actionable_advice.advice: If feeling lonely, seek to share in someone else's journey by finding other people who share your struggles.
actionable_advice.how_to: If wanting to run a 5 kilometer run, find someone else who also wants to run 5 kilometers and struggles to do so. There's an immediate connection there.

memorable_stories: Harley's story of starting a DJ business at age 13 because nobody would hire him.
Harley's story of his dad being arrested during his first month in college and then having to provide for his mom and siblings.
The story of Tobi Lutke, Shopify's founder, immigrating to Canada, starting a snowboard company, and developing the Shopify software as a better way to sell snowboards.
Harley's anecdote about Shopify wanting Supreme as a client because their flash sales would test and improve Shopify's infrastructure.
The story of matzo ball soup starting with a woman making soup for her friends and family, but then it becoming a business.
contrarian_claims: Having an 80-page business plan is not how most successful companies are created.
Success of a content creator is from creating a better version of existing products that their audience likes, not slapping their sticker on promotional material.
Don't believe people who say that you should not tell people your goals.
Vulnerability is a magnet, not a repellent.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Shopify President: How To Become A Millionaire For The Price Of A Starbucks Coffee!
